metrics.meta_title: Jetwing Travels - No.1 Travel Agent in Sri Lanka | Tour Operator in Sri Lanka
metrics.meta_description: Sri Lanka awaits! Book your unforgettable adventure with Jetwing Travels, the top-rated travel agent in Sri Lanka. Let our expert tour operator team plan your dream vacation today.

seo_structure.analysis: 1 H1 tag detected — correct and optimal for page focus. 8 H2 tags and 8 H3 tags create a well-structured hierarchy that supports both SEO and content scanability. The balanced distribution of subheadings across the page meets best practice standards for a commercial homepage.

messaging_clarity.analysis: Meta title is 76 characters — exceeding the optimal 50–60 character range by 16 characters. This risks truncation in SERPs on mobile devices. Meta description is 154 characters, falling within the optimal 120–160 range. With 1,706 words, the page exceeds typical landing page benchmarks (300–600 words) but is appropriate for a homepage showcasing multiple service offerings.

cta_usage.analysis: 11 CTAs detected — significantly exceeding the 1–3 standard for optimal conversion focus. This overabundance of call-to-action elements may dilute conversion intent and create decision paralysis for visitors. Consolidate or reorganise CTAs to 3–4 primary actions.

content_depth.analysis: 1,706 words is well above the 300–600 benchmark for landing pages and appropriate for a comprehensive travel agency homepage positioning multiple destinations and service categories. The heading structure (1 H1 + 8 H2 + 8 H3) supports content organisation at this length without sacrificing scannability.

ux_concerns.analysis: 28 of 477 images (5.9%) are missing alt text — an accessibility and SEO issue. While this percentage is relatively low, 28 unoptimised images represent missed opportunities for image search visibility and fail WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards. Prioritise adding descriptive alt text to all 28 images. The 92 internal links exceed the healthy 5–15 range, but this is typical for a travel operator homepage with many destination and service pages. All 15 external links should be audited for relevance and authority.

recommendations.title: 28 images missing alt text — accessibility and SEO gap
recommendations.reasoning: 5.9% of images (28 out of 477) lack alt text. While this is a relatively low percentage, with 477 images on the page, even this small gap represents a significant accessibility failure and SEO weakness. Every image should have descriptive alt text. Prioritize alt-text audits for high-impact images above the fold, then systematically add alt text to all remaining 28 images.

recommendations.title: 11 CTAs exceed standard focus limit for a landing page
recommendations.reasoning: The page has 11 call-to-action elements, well above the 1–3 standard for landing pages. This signals scattered conversion intent and dilutes the primary conversion goal. Consolidate CTAs to 2–3 primary actions (e.g., 'Book Now' and 'Request Quote') and demote secondary CTAs to footer or utility areas to sharpen page focus and improve conversion clarity.

recommendations.title: Meta title slightly exceeds optimal character length
recommendations.reasoning: The meta title 'Jetwing Travels - No.1 Travel Agent in Sri Lanka | Tour Operator in Sri Lanka' exceeds the optimal 50–60 character range. At approximately 77 characters, it will truncate in search results. Consider shortening to: 'Jetwing Travels | Top Travel Agent in Sri Lanka' (approximately 50 characters) to preserve visibility and maintain keyword relevance.

recommendations.title: 92 internal links signal potential over-linking and navigation confusion
recommendations.reasoning: With 92 internal links, the page far exceeds the healthy benchmark of 5–15 links. This level of internal linking dilutes page authority distribution, creates navigation clutter, and may confuse both users and search engines about primary page hierarchy. Audit and consolidate internal links to essential navigation and contextual references only, targeting 10–15 high-quality, purpose-driven links.

recommendations.title: Word count at upper range — consider structural refinement
recommendations.reasoning: At 1,706 words, the page sits at the upper limit of the 500–900 benchmark for service/product pages and approaches blog-post territory (1,200–2,000). For a landing page promoting travel services, this length risks information overload and reduced engagement. Review content hierarchy: identify and move lower-priority sections to dedicated service pages, or restructure with clearer scannable sections to improve user attention and conversion efficiency.

llm.system_prompt:
You are an expert SEO and UX analyst for a web agency. Analyse the given web page metrics and call the requested tool with your findings. Every insight and recommendation MUST cite a specific metric (e.g. exact word count, heading counts, CTA count, link counts, image alt-text percentage, meta tag presence). Be direct, specific, and non-generic — never write advice that could apply to any page.

INDUSTRY BENCHMARKS — use these to contextualise every finding:
- Word count: landing pages 300–600 words; service/product pages 500–900 words; blog posts 1,200–2,000 words
- H1 tags: exactly 1 is optimal; 0 = critical SEO gap; more than 1 dilutes page focus
- CTAs: 1–3 per page is standard; 0 = conversion gap; more than 5 may signal lack of focus
- Internal links: 5–15 is healthy; fewer than 3 may indicate weak site architecture
- Image alt text: 100% coverage is the target; any gap is an accessibility and SEO issue
- Meta description: 120–160 characters is optimal; missing means Google auto-generates it
- Meta title: 50–60 characters is optimal; missing or over 60 chars is an SEO issue

EXAMPLES — good insight vs bad insight:
  Good: 'The page has 0 H1 tags — a critical SEO issue and below the standard of exactly 1. Without an H1, search engines have no primary signal for the page topic. Add a single descriptive H1 above the fold immediately.'
  Bad:  'You should improve your headings for better SEO.' (no metric cited, applies to any page — never write this)

  Good: 'With 1 CTA detected, the page sits at the low end of the 1–3 standard for a service page. A single CTA limits conversion paths; add a secondary CTA lower on the page.'
  Bad:  'Add more calls to action to improve conversions.' (no metric cited, generic — never write this)
llm.user_prompt:
Audit this web page and call the requested tool with your findings.

**URL:** https://jetwingtravels.com/

**Extracted Metrics:**
- Word count: 1706
- Heading structure: h1: 1, h2: 8, h3: 8
- Call-to-action elements: 11
- Internal links: 92
- External links: 15
- Images: 477 total, 28 missing alt text (5.9%)
- Meta title: Jetwing Travels - No.1 Travel Agent in Sri Lanka | Tour Operator in Sri Lanka
- Meta description: Sri Lanka awaits! Book your unforgettable adventure with Jetwing Travels, the top-rated travel agent in Sri Lanka. Let our expert tour operator team plan your dream vacation today.

Every field must reference the specific numbers above.
